### Fix 3
Lines: 118, 123
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: FlexItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

```
  119                {t('Cancel')}

  (line 123):
  122            <FlexItem>
  123              <Button variant="primary" onClick={submit}>
  124                {t('Save')}

```

### Fix 4
Line: 12
Rule [sd-test-timepicker-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
TimePicker is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- TimePicker renders HelperTextItem which has a behavioral change: aria-hidden attribute removed from <span> in HelperTextItem
- TimePicker renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for TimePicker may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:

  localName: TimePicker
  module: @patternfly/react-core

```
   11    ModalVariant,
   12    TimePicker,
   13  } from '@patternfly/react-core';

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/monitoring-plugin/source/web/src/components/dashboards/shared/custom-time-range-modal.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1: Button.variant type changed
Lines 118 and 123 use `variant="secondary"` and `variant="primary"`. Both `"secondary"` and `"primary"` are still valid values in the new type (the only change is the addition of `"stateful"`). **No change needed.**

### Fix 2: Modal Family Migration
This is the most substantial change. The Modal component needs to be restructured:
- Remove `hasNoBodyWrapper` prop (line 86)
- Remove `showClose={false}` prop (line 89) — in v6, Modal doesn't show close by default unless `onClose` is provided
- Move `title={t('Custom time range')}` to a `<ModalHeader>` child component
- Wrap the body content in `<ModalBody>`
- Need to import `ModalBody` and `ModalHeader` from `@patternfly/react-core`

The current structure has `<Flex>` as direct child of `<Modal>`. In v6:
- `title` moves to `<ModalHeader title={...} />`
- Body content wraps in `<ModalBody>`

### Fix 3: Icon children to prop on Button
Lines 118 and 123 — looking at the Button contents: `{t('Cancel')}` and `{t('Save')}`. These are text-only, no icons present. **No change needed.**

### Fix 4: TimePicker behavioral changes
This is an informational/test-related fix about TimePicker's sub-component changes. The code itself doesn't need changing — it's about being aware of behavioral changes in tests. No test files are listed. **No code change needed.**

Now let me apply the changes:

arguments.after:
import {
  Button,
  DatePicker,
  Flex,
  FlexItem,
  Modal,
  ModalBody,
  ModalHeader,
  ModalVariant,
  TimePicker,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';

<Modal
      isOpen={isOpen}
      position="top"
      variant={ModalVariant.small}
    >
      <ModalHeader title={t('Custom time range')} />
      <ModalBody>
      <Flex className="custom-time-range-modal" direction={{ default: 'column' }}>
arguments.before:
<Modal
      hasNoBodyWrapper
      isOpen={isOpen}
      position="top"
      showClose={false}
      title={t('Custom time range')}
      variant={ModalVariant.small}
    >
      <Flex className="custom-time-range-modal" direction={{ default: 'column' }}>

content.text: Now let me verify the final result:

The file looks correct. Let me verify the structure is properly indented and semantically sound:

- **Line 87-91**: `<Modal>` with only the props that stay (`isOpen`, `position`, `variant`) — ✓
- **Line 92**: `<ModalHeader>` with the `title` prop moved from Modal — ✓
- **Lines 93-130**: `<ModalBody>` wrapping the entire `<Flex>` body content — ✓
- **Lines 11-12**: `ModalBody` and `ModalHeader` added to imports — ✓
- **Removed props**: `hasNoBodyWrapper`, `showClose={false}`, `title` all removed from `<Modal>` — ✓
- **Buttons**: `variant="secondary"` and `variant="primary"` are both still valid values — ✓
- **No icons** in the Button children (text only), so Fix 3 requires no change — ✓

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1: Button.variant type changed
**No change needed.** The values `"secondary"` (line 119) and `"primary"` (line 124) are both still valid in the new type. The only change to the union type was the addition of `"stateful"`, which doesn't affect existing usage.

### Fix 2: Modal Family Migration
**Applied.** Four changes made:
1. **Added imports**: `ModalBody` and `ModalHeader` added to the `@patternfly/react-core` import block (lines 11-12).
2. **Removed deprecated props**: `hasNoBodyWrapper` (line 86), `showClose={false}` (line 89), and `title={t('Custom time range')}` (line 90) removed from `<Modal>`.
3. **Added `<ModalHeader>`**: `<ModalHeader title={t('Custom time range')} />` added as first child of `<Modal>` (line 92), moving the `title` prop to the new component.
4. **Added `<ModalBody>`**: The `<Flex>` body content is now wrapped in `<ModalBody>` (lines 93 and 130). The `showClose={false}` behavior is the v6 default (no close button unless `onClose` is provided), so removal is correct.

### Fix 3: Icon children to prop on Button
**No change needed.** Both `<Button>` instances (lines 119 and 124) contain only text children (`{t('Cancel')}` and `{t('Save')}`), not icon elements. The rule only applies when `<Button>` contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`.

### Fix 4: TimePicker behavioral changes
**No change needed.** This is an informational fix about behavioral changes in TimePicker's rendered sub-components (HelperTextItem, Popper). The source code usage of `<TimePicker>` itself doesn't need modification — the fix advises updating tests if they exist. No companion test files were listed.

chunks.response:
The file looks correct. Let me verify the structure is properly indented and semantically sound:

- **Line 87-91**: `<Modal>` with only the props that stay (`isOpen`, `position`, `variant`) — ✓
- **Line 92**: `<ModalHeader>` with the `title` prop moved from Modal — ✓
- **Lines 93-130**: `<ModalBody>` wrapping the entire `<Flex>` body content — ✓
- **Lines 11-12**: `ModalBody` and `ModalHeader` added to imports — ✓
- **Removed props**: `hasNoBodyWrapper`, `showClose={false}`, `title` all removed from `<Modal>` — ✓
- **Buttons**: `variant="secondary"` and `variant="primary"` are both still valid values — ✓
- **No icons** in the Button children (text only), so Fix 3 requires no change — ✓

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1: Button.variant type changed
**No change needed.** The values `"secondary"` (line 119) and `"primary"` (line 124) are both still valid in the new type. The only change to the union type was the addition of `"stateful"`, which doesn't affect existing usage.

### Fix 2: Modal Family Migration
**Applied.** Four changes made:
1. **Added imports**: `ModalBody` and `ModalHeader` added to the `@patternfly/react-core` import block (lines 11-12).
2. **Removed deprecated props**: `hasNoBodyWrapper` (line 86), `showClose={false}` (line 89), and `title={t('Custom time range')}` (line 90) removed from `<Modal>`.
3. **Added `<ModalHeader>`**: `<ModalHeader title={t('Custom time range')} />` added as first child of `<Modal>` (line 92), moving the `title` prop to the new component.
4. **Added `<ModalBody>`**: The `<Flex>` body content is now wrapped in `<ModalBody>` (lines 93 and 130). The `showClose={false}` behavior is the v6 default (no close button unless `onClose` is provided), so removal is correct.

### Fix 3: Icon children to prop on Button
**No change needed.** Both `<Button>` instances (lines 119 and 124) contain only text children (`{t('Cancel')}` and `{t('Save')}`), not icon elements. The rule only applies when `<Button>` contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`.

### Fix 4: TimePicker behavioral changes
**No change needed.** This is an informational fix about behavioral changes in TimePicker's rendered sub-components (HelperTextItem, Popper). The source code usage of `<TimePicker>` itself doesn't need modification — the fix advises updating tests if they exist. No companion test files were listed.
